title: Layout Managers, Resize, Scaling, High DPI, and Reflow · Language Clinic: Layout Manager Scope
summary: The Canvas has fixed pixels, controls vanish on a small window, pack and grid conflict in one parent, and high-DPI text clips. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the layout manager scope increment in responsive desktop layout and resize policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use one geometry manager per parent and configure grid weights, sticky behavior, minimum sizes, and padding intentionally.
Verify item counts, coordinates, tags, layer order, changed topology, and native screenshot or inspection evidence separately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-layout-manager-scope — Use one geometry manager per parent and configure grid weights, sticky behavior, minimum sizes, and padding intentionally.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-layout-resize-scaling-3
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-layout-resize-scaling-3.
# Case 5798: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def worked_maze_layout_resize_scaling_3(available_width: int = 720, available_height: int = 540, grid_extent: int = 18, cell_size: int = 28, visible_controls: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "gridworked3",
        "topology5798",
        "search3dekrow",
        "eventworked35798",
        "access5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if min(available_width, available_height, grid_extent, cell_size) < 1:
        return False, cell_size, "layout-shape"
    if cell_size * grid_extent > min(available_width, available_height):
        return False, cell_size, "viewport-overflow"
    if visible_controls < 5:
        return False, visible_controls, "control-clipping"
    if grid_extent > 512:
        return False, grid_extent, "resize-budget"
    assert cell_size * grid_extent <= min(available_width, available_height)
    return True, cell_size, "responsive-maze-layout"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-layout-manager-scope-1a54z1
# Competency: maze-layout-manager-scope.
# Case 8367: a corrupted-state recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: all graph claims need an independent witness.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def evidence_maze_layout_manager_scope_1a54z1(available_width: int = 720, available_height: int = 540, grid_extent: int = 18, cell_size: int = 28, visible_controls: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1a54z1 = "1a54z1-1z45a1-8367"
    assert evidence_variant_1a54z1
    evidence_axes_1a54z1 = (
        "grid1a54z1",
        "topology8367",
        "search1z45a1",
        "event1a54z18367",
        "access83671a54z1",
        "recovery1z45a18367",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1a54z1)) == 6
    if min(available_width, available_height, grid_extent, cell_size) < 1:
        return False, cell_size, "layout-shape"
    if cell_size * grid_extent > min(available_width, available_height):
        return False, cell_size, "viewport-overflow"
    if visible_controls < 5:
        return False, visible_controls, "control-clipping"
    if grid_extent > 512:
        return False, grid_extent, "resize-budget"
    assert cell_size * grid_extent <= min(available_width, available_height)
    return True, cell_size, "responsive-maze-layout"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Layout Manager Scope: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory puzzle team handles case 9140 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to derive a responsive cell scale while retaining visible controls and valid geometry; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Use one geometry manager per parent and configure grid weights, sticky behavior, minimum sizes, and padding intentionally.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Layout Manager Scope model · Theory Layout Manager Sco….
steps.why: Retrieving Layout Manager Scope through the concrete responsive desktop layout and resize policy case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Layout Manager Scope model · Theory Layout Manager Sco…, keep Layout Manager Scope connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use one geometry manager per parent and configure grid weights, sticky behavior, minimum sizes, and padding intentionally.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Layout Manager Scope model · Theory Layout Manager Sco…, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Layout Manager Scope precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Layout Manager Scope model · Theory Layout Manager Sco…, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 9140 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to derive a responsive cell scale while retaining visible controls and valid geometry; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Use one geometry manager per parent and configure grid weights, sticky behavior, minimum sizes, and padding intentionally.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Layout Manager Scope model · Theory Layout Manager Sco…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Mixing pack and grid anywhere in one application is forbidden.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-layout-manager-scope-1a54z1.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-layout-manager-scope-1a54z1(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_layout_manager_scope_1a54z1\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?min\(available_width, available_height, grid_extent, cell_size\) < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?cell_size \* grid_extent > min\(available_width, available_height\))(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?visible_controls < 5)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?grid_extent > 512)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
